articles.title: Woman gives birth as flight from Jamaica lands at JFK Airport
articles.article_text: --- title: Woman gives birth as flight from Jamaica lands at JFK Airport url: https://abc7ny.com/post/woman-gives-birth-flight-jamaica-lands-jfk-airport-nyc/18847958/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: In an ATC recording, the ground controller asked if the baby was out yet and then suggested that the baby should named Kennedy. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['health'] tags: ['18847958', 'air-travel,birth,baby,Jamaica,Queens,New-York-City'] --- JAMAICA, Queens (WABC) -- A passenger on a Caribbean Airlines flight went into labor and gave birth as the plane approached JFK International Airport this weekend, the airline said. Flight BW005 from Kingston, Jamaica, landed at JFK just before noon on Saturday. The mother and newborn baby were attended to by medical personnel after landing to receive necessary care. In recordings from ATC.com, the pilot can be heard telling the controller they have a pregnant passenger who went into labor and requesting medical personnel at the gate. In another ATC recording, the ground controller asks if the baby is out yet and then suggests that the baby should named Kennedy. In a statement, Caribbean Airlines said, "The airline commends the professionalism and measured response of its crew, who managed the situation in accordance with established procedures, ensuring the safety and comfort of all onboard." The airline said no emergency was declared during the flight. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Parents charged after toddler injured by wolf at Hersheypark's ZooAmerica
articles.article_text: --- title: Parents charged after toddler injured by wolf at Hersheypark's ZooAmerica url: https://abc7ny.com/post/parents-charged-toddler-injured-wolf-hersheyparks-zooamerica/18848000/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: According to police, their toddler put his hand into a wolf enclosure over the weekend, and was injured by the wild animal. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['disasters-accidents'] tags: ['18848000', 'child-injured,toddler,Hersheypark,zoo,animal,wild-animals,u.s.-&-world,Pennsylvania'] --- HERSHEY, Pa. -- Two Pennsylvania parents are facing charges after an incident at Hersheypark's ZooAmerica. According to police, their toddler put his hand into a wolf enclosure over the weekend, and was injured by the wild animal. Carrie Sortor, 43, and Stephen Wilson, 61, are each charged with one count of endangering the welfare of a child. Investigators say the pair were about 25 to 30 feet away from their 17-month-old at the time of the incident, and appeared to be on their phones. According to officials, the child squeezed through a small opening in the wooden perimeter fence and reached through the primary fence enclosure, where a wolf grabbed the child's hand with its mouth. Several bystanders then intervened and helped pull the child away. The toddler suffered minor injuries.

articles.title: Supreme Court vacates Steve Bannon contempt-of-Congress charges
articles.author: Devin Dwyer
articles.article_text: --- title: Supreme Court vacates Steve Bannon contempt-of-Congress charges author: Devin Dwyer url: https://abc7ny.com/post/supreme-court-vacates-steve-bannon-contempt-congress-charges/18847878/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Steve Bannon refused to honor a subpoena to testify about the Jan. 6, 2021, attack. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['politics'] tags: ['abc-news, national, 18847878', 'steve-bannon,politics,u.s.-&-world,donald-trump,u.s.-supreme-court,supreme-court,abc-news,national'] --- Bannon refused to honor a subpoena to testify about the Jan. 6, 2021, attack. The Supreme Court on Monday vacated contempt-of-Congress charges against ex-Trump advisor Steve Bannon, who had refused to honor a subpoena from the committee investigating the Jan. 6, 2021, attack, and later served a four-month sentence. The Court did not explain its decision. There were no noted dissents. In a brief order, the Court noted that the Trump Justice Department has moved to drop the indictment against Bannon and returned the case to a lower court for dismissal. Bannon, who served as President Donald Trump's chief White House strategist during Trump's first term, served a four-month prison term after being found guilty on contempt charges following a July 2022 trial. A jury found Bannon guilty on two counts of contempt of Congress for defying a subpoena to testify about Jan. 6 before the House select committee that investigated the attack. In addition to serving prison time, Bannon paid a $6,500 fine. This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

articles.title: 7 On Your Side: Single mom gets back stolen disability benefits
articles.author: Nina Pineda
articles.article_text: --- title: 7 On Your Side: Single mom gets back stolen disability benefits author: Nina Pineda url: https://abc7ny.com/post/7-side-single-mom-gets-back-stolen-disability-benefits/18846955/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: After her disability benefits were stolen, a single mom reached out to 7 On Your Side and Nina Pineda for help. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18846955', '7-On-Your-Side,disability,disability-issues,social-security,finance,theft,fraud'] --- A local single mother struggling with health issues waited years to qualify for disability, only to have her lump sum benefits stolen. Without that lifeline, her family had to move out of their home. Struggling to keep herself and her three kids afloat, she turned to Nina Pineda and 7 On Your Side for help. "My health started going downhill, really fast. I can't get around that well anymore. I have a mobile cane. Sometimes I use a roller walker. I have spinal issues, so I can't work anymore." Kimberly Mays has always worked to take care of her twin boys and her daughter. But in 2019, her life took a drastic turn. Just as the world went into COVID lockdown, Kimberly applied for disability benefits -- but it was no easy battle. After years of proving her illness, she finally was approved to receive a lump sum payment through direct deposit. "Why did you not want it to come in the mail?" Nina asked Kimberly. "Because it's a large check. $66,000. It's literally four years worth of backpay," Kimberly explained. "They mailed it anyway." Which is exactly how her check was stolen. "It's not fair. And I have a whole bunch of health issues. And that money was supposed to help me and my children," Kimberly said. Without the funds for her family, they were forced to move into a more affordable apartment. It wasn't even furnished the first time Nina met the family. "My sons just turned 18. They're twins and we have nothing," Kimberly said. After a tough year of trying to keep up with her health and provide for her kids, she finally decided to call us. "I saw a few stories that you've done recently. You sat there, you listened to them, you showed empathy. And I'm sorry, I'm tired of not being listened to," she said. 7 On Your Side contacted the social security administration, which said they turned Kimberly's case over to the U.S. Treasury. The Treasury Department apologized for the delay and finally deposited the money into Kimberly's account directly. "I am so grateful to you and your staff and the whole Channel 7. I finally got my back pay," Kimberly said. With $66,082, a fresh start; beginning with beds, a couch, and decorations for their new home. "Thank you very much. Thank you so much from the bottom of my heart," Kimberly said. ---------- SHARE YOUR STORY Do you have an issue with a company that you haven't been able to resolve? If so, 7 On Your Side wants to help you! Fill out the form below or email your questions, issues, or story ideas by filling out the form below or by emailing 7OnYourSideNina@abc.com. All emails MUST INCLUDE YOUR NAME AND CELLPHONE NUMBER. Without a phone number, 7 On Your Side will not be able to respond.

articles.title: Amazon, UPS, FedEx, USPS add temporary fuel surcharges to offset extra costs
articles.author: Suzanne Phan
articles.article_text: --- title: Amazon, UPS, FedEx, USPS add temporary fuel surcharges to offset extra costs author: Suzanne Phan url: https://abc7ny.com/post/war-iran-amazon-ups-fedex-usps-add-temporary-fuel-surcharges-offset-extra-costs/18846222/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The rising fuel costs amid the war in Iran mean it will cost you more to get packages delivered. Starting April 17, Amazon will apply new surcharges to its sellers. UPS, FedEx, and USPS have also added fuel surcharges to counter rising fuel costs. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['society'] tags: ['18846222', 'economy,u.s.-&-world,iran,israel,war,gas-prices,amazon,UPS,fedex,USPS,inflation,San-Francisco'] --- SAN FRANCISCO -- The rising fuel costs amid the war in Iran mean it will cost you more to get packages delivered. Starting April 17, Amazon will apply new surcharges to its sellers. UPS, FedEx, and USPS have also added fuel surcharges to counter rising fuel costs. Rising fuel prices mean sticker shock at the pump for many drivers. "It's $137 dollars. That's significant," said Jill from Pleasant Hill. That's how much it cost her to gas up on Sunday. The Strait of Hormuz has remained closed since the U.S. and Israel began their war with Iran. It's a critical shipping route for crude exports from major oil producers. The closure has pushed oil prices higher and driven up gasoline costs. LIVE UPDATES: Get the latest on war in Iran here To counter rising fuel costs, Amazon plans to tack a temporary 3.5% fuel surcharge on its third-party sellers. "Transportation costs are a big factor there," said Dr. Dima Leschinskii, a professor of Finance at Menlo College. "Every company that is involved and has logistics and they have to pay for gas, either they have to absorb this cost, or they will charge the third party that will provide this service. I'm not surprised this is happening, because at some point, Amazon will say we cannot absorb all this cost." The e-commerce giant is following in the footsteps of UPS, FedEx and the U.S. Postal Service. Starting April 26, the USPS is slapping a temporary 8% price hike on certain packages, including priority mail shipments, because of increasing fuel prices. That means customers may have to pay more for deliveries. "That's crazy," said Stevie, a driver from San Francisco. "It's going to slow people down from ordering stuff. Most definitely because you are already paying other costs on top of it." "One way or another, this is going to ripple through the supply chain and ripple through our pocketbooks," said Professor Andy Tsay, a supply chain expert at Santa Clara University Leavey School of Business. "The higher oil prices are affecting shipping and logistics costs. Amazon, UPA, USPS. I don't think there's any escape. Mortgage rates are going up. Everyone is talking about affordability since before the presidential election and the geopolitical situation isn't making things any better." Economic experts say as gas prices continue to rise, food delivery apps are also likely to consider fuel-related surcharges.

articles.title: 'Beverly Hills, 90210' alum Tori Spelling, several children involved in car crash in California
articles.author: Shafiq Najib
articles.article_text: --- title: 'Beverly Hills, 90210' alum Tori Spelling, several children involved in car crash in California author: Shafiq Najib url: https://abc7ny.com/post/tori-spelling-several-children-involved-car-crash-temecula-california-authorities-say/18845270/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: "Beverly Hills, 90210" star Tori Spelling was involved in a two-vehicle crash in Temecula, California, officials briefed on the incident told ABC News. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-05 categories: ['disasters-accidents'] tags: ['no-otrc, 18845270', 'tori-spelling,car-crash,california,entertainment'] --- Authorities say a two-vehicle crash occurred Thursday evening in Temecula. TEMECULA, Calif. -- Actress Tori Spelling was involved in a two-vehicle crash Thursday evening in Temecula, California, officials briefed on the incident told ABC News. The Riverside County Sheriff's Office said that a crash occurred at approximately 5:44 p.m. PT and that everyone involved was medically evaluated following the crash. No arrests have been made and the cause of the crash remains under investigation. TMZ was the first to report the news. According to TMZ, the "Beverly Hills 90210" alum and seven children -- her own children and their friends -- were transported to the hospital in multiple ambulances with injuries described as mainly cuts and bruises. ABC News has reached out to Spelling's representative for comment, but did not hear back immediately. Spelling is the mother of five children, Liam, Stella, Hattie, Finn and Beau, whom she shares with ex-husband Dean McDermott. The couple married in 2006 and later starred in the reality series "Tori & Dean: Home Sweet Hollywood," which aired from 2007 to 2012. According to a divorce petition, the couple separated in June 2023.

articles.title: AccuWeather: Breezy and cooler
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: AccuWeather: Breezy and cooler author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/nyc-weather-forecast-7-day-temperatures/27410/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Monday will be brisk, with highs around 56 degrees and a noticeable chill in the air. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2020-02-12 categories: ['weather'] tags: ['nyc weather, accuweather forecast, new york city weather, weather forecast, tri-state weather, heat wave, hot, air quality, air quality alert, sam champion, lee goldberg, jeff smith, brittany bell, dani beckstrom, 27410', 'weather,accuweather,forecast,nyc-weather,rain,snow,cold,heat,spring,summer,fall,winter,storm,drought,New-York-City'] --- AccuWeather forecast for NYC, New York, New Jersey and Connecticut NEW YORK (WABC) -- Monday is brisk, with highs around 56 degrees and a noticeable chill in the air. Tuesday looks even cooler, with highs near 52 degrees and a more wintry feel due to a mix of clouds and sunshine. A passing disturbance could produce a stray rain shower, and in higher elevations north and west of the city, that could even mix with soft snow pellets or a few snowflakes. The Catskills, in particular, could see a very light coating at the highest elevations. Wednesday stays bright but cool, with highs holding near 51 degrees. After that, temperatures begin a gradual rebound, returning to the 60s on Thursday. By the end of the week, spring makes a stronger comeback, with sunshine and highs pushing into the lower 70s - shaping up to be a beautiful spring day about a week from now. Monday Breezy and cooler. High 56. Tuesday Breezy Blend. High 50. Wednesday Bright but staying cool. High 49. Thursday Warmer sunshine. High 54. Friday Even warmer. High 67. Saturday Sunny and pleasant. High 71. Sunday More clouds than sun. High 66. Please fill out the form below to submit weather photos and videos. MORE ACCUWEATHER RESOURCES Check AccuTrack Radar Air Quality Tracker NWS Advisories, Watches and Warnings School closings and delays For weather updates wherever you go, please download the AccuWeather app. Follow chief meteorologist Lee Goldberg, Sam Champion, meteorologist Brittany Bell, meteorologist Jeff Smith, and meteorologist Dani Beckstrom on social media.

articles.title: Artemis II astronauts race to set a new distance record from Earth and behold the moon's far side
articles.author: MARCIA DUNN
articles.article_text: --- title: Artemis II astronauts race to set a new distance record from Earth and behold the moon's far side author: MARCIA DUNN url: https://abc7ny.com/post/artemis-ii-astronauts-race-set-new-distance-record-earth-behold-moons-far-side/18847214/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['science'] tags: ['18847214', 'moon,u.s.-&-world,space,nasa,science,technology'] --- HOUSTON -- With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye. The six-hour flyby is the highlight of NASA's first return to the moon since the Apollo era with three Americans and one Canadian - a step toward landing boot prints near the moon's south pole in just two years. A prize - and bragging rights - awaits Artemis II. Less than an hour before kicking off the fly-around and intense lunar observations, the four astronauts were set to become the most distant humans in history, surpassing the distance record of 248,655 miles (400,171 kilometers) set by Apollo 13 in April 1970. Mission Control expected Artemis II to surpass that record by more than 4,100 miles (6,600 kilometers). The astronauts woke up to the voice of Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just months before his death last August. "Welcome to my old neighborhood," said Lovell, who also flew on Apollo 8, humanity's first lunar visit. "It's a historic day and I know how busy you'll be, but don't forget to enjoy the view." Artemis II is using the same maneuver that Apollo 13 did after its "Houston, we've had a problem" oxygen tank explosion wiped out any hope of a moon landing. Known as a free-return lunar trajectory, this no-stopping-to-land route takes advantage of Earth and the moon's gravity, reducing the need for fuel. It's a celestial figure-eight that will put the astronauts on course for home, once they emerge from behind the moon Monday evening. Commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, Christina Koch and Canada's Jeremy Hansen were on track to pass as close as 4,070 miles (6,550 kilometers) to the moon, as their Orion capsule whips past it, hangs a U-turn and then heads back toward Earth. It will take them four days to get back, with a splashdown in the Pacific concluding their test flight on Friday. Their expected speed at closest approach to the moon: 3,139 mph (5,052 kph). Wiseman and his crew spent years studying lunar geography to prepare for the big event, adding solar eclipses to their repertoire during the past few weeks. By launching last Wednesday, they ensured themselves of a total solar eclipse from their vantage point behind the moon, courtesy of the cosmos. Topping their science target list: Orientale Basin, a sprawling impact basin with three concentric rings, the outermost of which stretches nearly 600 miles (950 kilometers) across. Other sightseeing goals: the Apollo 12 and 14 landing sites from 1969 and 1971, respectively, as well as fringes of the south polar region, the preferred locale for future touchdowns. Farther afield, Mercury, Venus, Mars and Saturn - not to mention Earth - will be visible. Their moon mentor, NASA geologist Kelsey Young, expects thousands of pictures. "People all over the world connect with the moon. This is something that every single person on this planet can understand and connect with," she said on the eve of the flyby, wearing eclipse earrings. Artemis II is NASA's first astronaut moonshot since Apollo 17 in 1972. It sets the stage for next year's Artemis III, which will see another Orion crew practice docking with lunar landers in orbit around Earth. The culminating moon landing by two astronauts near the moon's south pole will follow on Artemis IV in 2028. While Artemis II may be taking Apollo 13's path, it's most reminiscent of Apollo 8 and humanity's first lunar visitors who orbited the moon on Christmas Eve 1968 and read from the Book of Genesis. Glover said flying to the moon during Christianity's Holy Week brought home for him "the beauty of creation." Earth is an oasis amid "a whole bunch of nothing, this thing we call the universe" where humanity exists as one, he observed over the weekend. "This is an opportunity for us to remember where we are, who we are, and that we are the same thing and that we've got to get through this together," Glover said, clasping hands with his crewmates.

articles.title: New Jersey state troopers rescue bear cub from highway ditch
articles.article_text: --- title: New Jersey state troopers rescue bear cub from highway ditch url: https://abc7ny.com/story/new-jersey-state-troopers-rescue-bear-cub-highway-ditch-union-township/18847727/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: State police troopers came to the rescue of a bear cub found in a ditch along the side of a major interstate highway in northern New Jersey. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['pets-animals'] tags: ['18847727', 'bear,animal-rescue,state-troopers,New-Jersey'] --- UNION TOWNSHIP, New Jersey -- State police troopers came to the rescue of a bear cub found in a ditch along the side of a major interstate highway in northern New Jersey. Troopers from the Perryville station responded shortly before 1:40 p.m. Wednesday to milepost 12.2 on I-78 eastbound in Union Township. The animal was by itself, officials said. The bear was soon safely secured and taken back to the state police barracks, where it was later turned over to staffers with the state's Environmental Protection Department, who were caring for the animal. It's not clear how the cub ended up in the ditch or how long it had been there before it was spotted. Details on the bear's condition were not available Friday.

articles.title: Here and Now: Lincoln Center reimagines Ntozake Shange's choreopoem on its 50th anniversary
articles.author: Sandra Bookman; Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Here and Now: Lincoln Center reimagines Ntozake Shange's choreopoem on its 50th anniversary author: Sandra Bookman; Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/here-now-sandra-bookman-lincoln-center-reimagines-ntozake-shanges-choreopoem-50th-anniversary/18835597/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: This year marks the 50th anniversary of Ntozake Shange's powerful choreopoem, "For Colored Girls Who Have Considered Suicide / When the Rainbow Is Enuf." sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['community-events'] tags: ['18835597', 'here-and-now,lincoln-center,performing-arts,black-history,music,nonprofit,health,health-care,mental-health,womens-health,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK (WABC) -- Coming up on Here and Now, a classic choreopoem for girls of color is reimagined with music at Lincoln Center. This year marks the 50th anniversary of Ntozake Shange's powerful choreopoem, "For Colored Girls Who Have Considered Suicide / When the Rainbow Is Enuf." To mark this milestone, Lincoln Center's American Songbook series is presenting a re-imagined, musical treatment of the classic. It tells the stories of seven women who have suffered the indignities of a racist and sexist society. Joining us to discuss is composer, playwright and performer Natalie Brown. Also ahead, learn more about Samaritan Daytop Village, a nationally-recognized human services organization changing lives for more than 60 years. Plus, the initiative providing much-needed support for grandparents now raising their grandchildren. More on the poster exhibit bringing early Black stage and screen productions back to life. And meet the co-directors behind "Meal Ticket," a new documentary telling the story of McDonald's all-American games. Here and Now with Sandra Bookman airs Sundays at 12:00 p.m. on Channel 7, WABC-TV in New York City. WATCH: More Here and Now episodes SEND STORY IDEAS TO EYEWITNESS NEWS

articles.title: Watch 'In the Fast Lane:' An inside look at the New York International Auto Show
articles.article_text: --- title: Watch 'In the Fast Lane:' An inside look at the New York International Auto Show url: https://abc7ny.com/post/new-york-international-auto-show-preview-watch-fast-lane-wabc-tv-abc7ny/18787890/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Join Eyewitness News meteorologists Lee Goldberg and Brittany Bell and sports anchor Ryan Field for an inside look at the New York International Auto Show in our special 'In the Fast Lane.' sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-03-27 categories: ['automotive'] tags: ['18787890', 'new-york-auto-show,cars,New-York-City'] --- Watch 'In the Fast Lane' wherever you stream ABC 7 New York. NEW YORK (WABC) -- Join Eyewitness News meteorologists Lee Goldberg and Brittany Bell and sports anchor Ryan Field for an inside look at the New York International Auto Show in our special, "In the Fast Lane," wherever you stream ABC 7 New York. The New York International Auto Show is buzzing with all the newest and hottest cars, and ABC7 takes you on an exciting ride to see it all. This year, there's a brand-new ride experience plus an expanded electric vehicle test track with more cars than ever. And several historic exhibits that take you back in time, for a fascinating look at automotive innovation. Then buckle up for thrilling rides, including test drives in the brand-new Bentley Continental GT Speed and stunning 2027 Kia Telluride. And come along for a once-in-a-lifetime spin in a million-dollar car! Go inside the dazzling world of "restomod" cars, which restore and modify the base of older cars with new conveniences - while preserving vintage charm. Plus, get a taste of off-road adventure at Camp Jeep outside Javits, as we scale Jeep Mountain. And we'll show you an exclusive sneak peek of the upcoming film "DRIFTER," starring filmmaker and car enthusiast Sung Kang. Kang is known for his role as Han in "The Fast and the Furious" film franchise. His new movie showcases the competitive drifting scene, and visitors to the NY Auto Show will get an up-close look at the actual cars used in the film. Plus, check out the EV Test Track made just for kids, an inspiring new feature to help you relax inside your car, and a heartwarming story of using new vehicles to help feed the hungry in the New York area. Gear up for your guide to everything cutting-edge in the world of wheels! Only ABC7 brings it to you. Take a ride "In the Fast Lane!" Watch a re-broadcast on ABC 7 on Sunday, April 5 at 4:00 a.m. Discover more New York auto show coverage at abc7ny.com/nyautoshow. ---------- * Get Eyewitness News Delivered * Download our connected TV app * More local news * Send us a news tip * Download the abc7NY app for breaking news alerts on the go

articles.title: Up Close: Republican nominee Bruce Blakeman on New York gubernatorial race
articles.author: Bill Ritter; Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Up Close: Republican nominee Bruce Blakeman on New York gubernatorial race author: Bill Ritter; Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/close-bill-ritter-republican-nominee-bruce-blakeman-ny-gubernatorial-race/18835398/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: New polls show the race for governor of New York narrowing a bit. Democrat incumbent Kathy Hochul still has a big lead over Republican gubernatorial candidate Bruce Blakeman. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['politics'] tags: ['18835398', 'up-close,politics,governor,new-york-governor,kathy-hochul,democrats,republicans,new-york-state-politics,New-York,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK CITY (WABC) -- On this edition of Up Close, we discuss the latest on the race for New York governor. New polls show the race for governor of New York narrowing a bit. Democrat incumbent Kathy Hochul still has a big lead over Republican gubernatorial candidate Bruce Blakeman. Blakeman is the current Nassau County Executive and a staunch backer of President Donald Trump. We speak with him about the race and what he would do as governor. Meanwhile, Mayor Zohran Mamdani is in a big push for taxes on big corporations in New York City. The push is to help pay for his proposed programs and to make up for the over $5 billion deficit. So, how is the critically important business community reacting to all this? And will some of them decide to move a portion or all of their business to low-tax states? This morning, we talk to Steve Fulop, the former mayor of Jersey City, who is now the president of Partnership for New York City. The group represents businesses in the country's biggest city. Up Close airs Sunday mornings at 11:00 a.m. on Channel 7, WABC-TV in New York City. RELATED: Watch more episodes of Up Close

articles.title: Tiempo: Impact of US trial against ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro
articles.author: Joe Torres; Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Tiempo: Impact of US trial against ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro author: Joe Torres; Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/tiempo-joe-torres-impact-us-trial-ousted-venezuelan-president-nicolas-maduro/18835241/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The former president of Venezuela and his wife Cilia Flores faced a judge in a New York court last month after their dramatic capture by U.S. forces in Caracas back in January. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['community-events'] tags: ['18835241', 'tiempo,politics,venezuela,nicolás-maduro,trial,u.s.-&-world,New-York-City'] --- NEW YORK CITY (WABC) -- On this edition of Tiempo, we discuss the impact of the U.S. trial against ousted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. The former president of Venezuela and his wife Cilia Flores faced a judge in a New York court last month after their dramatic capture by U.S. forces in Caracas back in January. There were demonstrators outside the courthouse. Meanwhile, Venezuela remains in a state of major political transition. Joining us to discuss is Alejandro Velasco, a professor at NYU and native of Venezuela. Also ahead, New Jersey law now bans ICE agents and police from wearing masks on the job in the Garden State. It's one of three new laws signed recently by Gov. Mikie Sherrill in response to the Trump administration's immigration crackdown. We'll have more details on the new pieces of legislation coming up. Tiempo airs Sunday mornings at 11:30 on Channel 7, WABC-TV in New York City. MORE: Watch more episodes of Tiempo here! Tiempo airs Sunday mornings at 11:30 a.m. on Channel 7.

articles.title: Missing sisters found after last seen leaving home in Coney Island
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Missing sisters found after last seen leaving home in Coney Island author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/coney-island-missing-sisters-police-searching-2-young-girls-last-seen-leaving-home-brooklyn-nyc/18843553/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Two young sisters who were reported missing in Brooklyn returned to their home on Sunday night. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-05 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18843553', 'missing-girl,missing-person,investigation,police,nypd,Coney-Island,Brooklyn,New-York-City'] --- CONEY ISLAND, Brooklyn (WABC) -- Two young sisters who went missing from their home in Brooklyn returned on Sunday night. The 11 and 12-year-ol girls were last seen leaving their home on West 25th Street in Coney Island around 12:30 a.m. on Saturday morning. Few other details were known. ---------- * Get Eyewitness News Delivered * Download our connected TV app * More local news * Send us a news tip * Download the abc7NY app for breaking news alerts on the go Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? articles.title: Driver charged in deadly hit-and-run that killed man in Freeport
articles.author: Eyewitness News
articles.article_text: --- title: Driver charged in deadly hit-and-run that killed man in Freeport author: Eyewitness News url: https://abc7ny.com/post/freeport-deadly-hit-run-driver-charged-man-struck-killed-nassau-county-long-island/18844153/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Michael Bonner, 40, is charged with leaving the scene of an incident resulting in death and tampering with physical evidence in the deadly incident in Nassau County. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-05 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18844153', 'hit-and-run,driver,arrest,pedestrian-struck,pedestrian-killed,crime,police,nassau-county-police,nassau-county-news,Freeport,Nassau-County,New-York'] --- FREEPORT, New York (WABC) -- Police have arrested and charged a driver in a hit-and-run that killed a man on Long Island last month. Michael Bonner, 40, is charged with leaving the scene of an incident resulting in death and tampering with physical evidence in the deadly incident in Nassau County. The hit-and-run happened on Merrick Road in Freeport around 8 p.m. on March 25. Nassau County Police say 66-year-old Narciso Guerra Lopez was crossing the road when he was struck by Bonner's car. First responders transported the victim to the hospital in critical condition. Lopez later succumbed to his injuries and died at the hospital. Bonner will be arraigned on Sunday at the First District Court in Hempstead. ---------- * Get Eyewitness News Delivered * Download our connected TV app * More local news * Send us a news tip * Download the abc7NY app for breaking news alerts on the go Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? articles.title: Long Island man accused of possessing enough Fentanyl to kill 2 million people
articles.article_text: --- title: Long Island man accused of possessing enough Fentanyl to kill 2 million people url: https://abc7ny.com/post/long-island-man-accused-dealing-drugs-could-kill-2-million-people-suffolk-district-attorney-says/18830959/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: A man on Long Island was indicted on Thursday on charges of dealing drugs that could kill 2 million people. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-02 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18830959', 'long-island,drug,drug-arrest,cocaine,Suffolk-County'] --- SUFFOLK COUNTY (WABC) -- A man on Long Island was indicted on Thursday on charges of dealing drugs that could kill 2 million people. Phillip Gonzales was indicted on 25 counts and is accused of operating as a major drug trafficker in Suffolk County. The Suffolk County District Attorney says they took a cache of weapons and illegal drugs, including cocaine and other drugs. The investigation started with a drug-related death last month in East Patchogue. Then an undercover investigation was launched so detectives could 'buy and bust,' which led to Gonzales' arrest. "The amount of drugs we seized in this case would be capable of causing the overdoses of 2.2 million people," DA Raymond Tierney said. The 42-year-old had about 7 kilograms of controlled substances and two loaded guns when he was arrested in February, according to the District Attorney's office. Suffolk Police Commissioner Kevin Catalina says his investigators led to Gonzales after two brothers overdosed in their East Patchogue home, and one of those young men died. "When we get one of these overdoses, quite frankly, we treat it like a homicide," Catalina said. The other man survived the overdose and was treated at the hospital. Investigators say they found glass bottles of cocaine and fentanyl in the brother's home. "You know, it's almost impossible to find somebody in Suffolk County who has not been either directly or indirectly affected by this issue," Catalina said. Also recovered in the home were cell phones that linked Gonzales to the sale of the drugs to the brothers, according to Tierney. Gonzales faces 15 years to life in prison if convicted. He will be arraigned on Monday because he still needs to retain a lawyer. Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Teen DWI arrests increasing on Long Island: 'Kids are partying more and starting earlier'
articles.author: Krista McNally
articles.article_text: --- title: Teen DWI arrests increasing on Long Island: 'Kids are partying more and starting earlier' author: Krista McNally url: https://abc7ny.com/post/long-island-police-experts-concerned-increase-teen-drunk-driving-arrests-nassau-suffolk-counties/18828701/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: Officials warn that the dangers are real and experts say alcohol kills more people a year than fentanyl. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-02 categories: ['crime-safety'] tags: ['18828701', 'dwi,underage-drinking,arrest,Nassau-County,Suffolk-County'] --- LONG ISLAND, New York (WABC) -- Experts are sounding the alarm about the increase in the number of teen drivers arrested for drunk driving on Long Island. "All the wonderful things are all gone like that--in an instant you make a decision that takes someone else's life," Nassau Police Commissioner Patrick Ryder said. Suffolk County is seeing a massive surge in underage DWI arrests: a 110% increase from 2023 to 2025. Over in Nassau, there has been a 13% increase from 2024 to 2025, according to newly released data from both police departments. "We are seeing an increase," Ryder said. "Kids are partying more and starting earlier." Some of those DWI crashes have turned deadly. "You saw what happened to Officer Patricia Espinosa," Ryder said. "She was killed because someone was stupid enough to get behind the wheel drunk." A 20-year-old alleged drunk driver struck Espinosa while she was on her way to work last month. Espinosa's death is renewing attention on the dangers of drunk driving. That's why experts say prevention is critical. "The key is delaying experimentation as long as possible," Dr. Jared Pachter, the addiction medicine fellowship director at Stony Brook Medicine, said. Pachter said that car accidents are the biggest risk with young people and alcohol use. "I was 19 at a family party and I was drinking everyone's drinks off the table and I did not stop," Briyenna Souter said. Souter said she started drinking as a teenager and quickly became addicted to alcohol. "I wanted to live that teenage life, and I went full steam ahead," Souter said. Nearly a decade later, Souter began her recovery at The Family and Children's Association where she works as an outreach coordinator helping others. She said what she's seeing now is alarming---kids are starting to drink younger and younger. "If you're drinking underage or even above age, people are there to help you through the process of being recovered," Souter said. Officials warn that the dangers are real---alcohol kills more people a year than fentanyl, according to Dr. Jeffrey Reynolds. "We need to make sure young people are having these conversations and getting the care they need," Reynolds said. "There is hope we can reverse this trend." ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: Bronx woman worried about health after apartment overrun with rats
articles.author: Anthony Carlo
articles.article_text: --- title: Bronx woman worried about health after apartment overrun with rats author: Anthony Carlo url: https://abc7ny.com/post/bronx-woman-worried-health-apartment-overrun-rats/18842006/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: After repeated calls for help to her landlord and complaints to the city, she is now worried about her health. sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-05 categories: ['health'] tags: ['18842006', 'rats,apartment,Mount-Hope,New-York-City,Bronx'] --- MT. HOPE, The Bronx (WABC) -- A woman from the Bronx is fighting a losing battle-she says her apartment is overrun with rats. After repeated calls for help to her landlord and complaints to the city, she is now worried about her health. Cora Robinson - armed with a piece of plywood - spends her days trying to defend her home against rats. "I thought it would keep them from running around the baseboard, but now what they are doing - they're so big, they jump over it," Robinson said. Robinson says at least two rats were rummaging around her kitchen on Friday night. Any hope of hosting for Easter went right out the window. "I'm tired of cleaning poop off the stove. I mean, if you turn the oven on, it smells like them," she says. Robinson says it is wearing her down. "I can't sleep, I'm not eating. And if I don't stop this, I'm going to wind up in the hospital. I almost wound up there yesterday," she adds. The breast cancer survivor already deals with health issues and is worried about what she and her dog are exposed to. "Her food and her bed and her water are in my bedroom. I can't leave it out here because they'll get into it. I'm just done. I'm just so disgusted because it's not right that I should have to live like this," she says. She has filed complaints with the city's Department of Housing Preservation and Development, which, according to documents, has cited the landlord in the past. Eyewitness News left a message for Asden Management and emailed the landlords, all of whom were the respondents in a tenant lawsuit in 2023 that alleged unlivable conditions at 1777 Grand Concourse. The tenants' lawyer says the case is still pending. Robinson says she would love to just move out of her building, but at 71, she just can't afford to. She says something is very wrong in this city if a senior can't live comfortably. "All these people need to come and take a look and smell. That's what I wish they would do," she says. ---------- * Get Eyewitness News Delivered * Download our connected TV app * More local news * Send us a news tip * Download the abc7NY app for breaking news alerts on the go Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? articles.title: Neglected dog 'left to die' on side of road in New Jersey, rescuers say
articles.article_text: --- title: Neglected dog 'left to die' on side of road in New Jersey, rescuers say url: https://abc7ny.com/post/monmouth-county-spca-cares-abandoned-dog-left-side-road-marlboro-nj/18834846/ hostname: abc7ny.com description: The shelter said the pup "was discarded like trash, unable to walk and skeletal from starvation." sitename: abc7ny.com date: 2026-04-03 categories: ['pets-animals'] tags: ['18834846', 'dog,pets,shelter,animal-rescue,Monmouth-County,Marlboro'] --- MONMOUTH COUNTY, New Jersey (WABC) -- The Monmouth County SPCA is helping to care for a neglected dog that they say was left to die on the side of a road in Marlboro. Shelter officials said they found Firefox left in a crate and unable to walk earlier this week. He weighed only 3 pounds when he was rescued. In a Facebook post, the shelter said the pup "was discarded like trash, unable to walk and skeletal from starvation." He had a body condition score of one, which is the lowest possible rating before death. Firefox's blood sugar levels are four times the normal limit due to trauma. Rescuers said he survived his first critical night in emergency care, but his road to recovery is long, as he is experiencing medical issues on top of starvation and neglect. Monmouth County SPCA is seeking donations, as Firefox has a long road to recovery. They said they need extra support for his hospitalization, physical therapy, specialized food regimen for weight gain and ongoing medical care. While law enforcement searches for the person responsible for abandoning Firefox, the shelter is asking if anyone knows where Firefox came from to reach out to the Marlboro Police Department or email them at info@monmouthcountyspca.org. ---------- Have a breaking news tip or an idea for a story we should cover? Send it to Eyewitness News using the form below. If attaching a video or photo, terms of use apply.

articles.title: NYPD officer shoots man after he charged at police with knife in East Harlem
articles.author: Alexa Herrera; Andrew Ramos; Aziza Shuler
articles.article_text: --- title: NYPD officer shoots man after he charged at police with knife in East Harlem author: Alexa Herrera; Andrew Ramos; Aziza Shuler url: https://www.cbsnews.com/newyork/news/nypd-man-shot-east-harlem/ hostname: cbsnews.com description: A man was shot after the NYPD said he charged at officers with a kitchen knife Monday morning outside of an East Harlem grocery store. sitename: CBS New York date: 2026-04-06 categories: ['Local News'] tags: ['Harlem, NYPD, East Harlem'] --- A man was shot after the NYPD said he outside of an East Harlem grocery store.The early Monday morning after a 53-year-old got into a verbal fight with another person inside the City Fresh Market on East 120th Street and Third Avenue.Multiple employees had to physically remove him from the store, the NYPD said. The man returned to the store a short time later with a large kitchen knife in his hand, banging on the window, according to police. Surveillance video shows the man wielding a 13-inch knife, yelling, pressing against the store's glass and ripping down signs. Employees locked the door and called 911 just after 1:30 a.m. When officers arrived, they told the man to drop the knife multiple times, but he "quickly advanced toward the officers with the knife extended out." An officer shot him in the abdomen. He was taken to Harlem Hospital and is in stable condition, police said. Supermarket worker Victor Salvada said staff knew the man who often shoplifted. But this was the first time things turned violent. One man who works in the neighborhood said it can be a dangerous area. "You don't know what's going to happen day or night," he said. "A lot of people walking around, you don't know what they're gonna do. Pretty worrying." The responding officers were taken to a local hospital to be evaluated, but were not injured. The NYPD recovered the knife at the scene as they continue to investigate.
